gaps.title: Credits route silently discards the engine's `channel` — MMS billed at 1x instead of 3x, RCS not flat-priced
gaps.detail: The engine deliberately sends the channel for rate-card pricing: worker.rs:548-552 builds CreditReserveRequest with `channel: Some("rcs" | doc_channel)` (doc_channel can be "mms"), and types.rs:203-207 documents "channel the rate card should price ('sms'/'mms'/'rcs'; RCS is flat 1 credit)". The TS type mirrors it (src/lib/sabsms/types.ts:521-522). But route.ts:80-87 hardcodes `channel: 'sms'` with a now-false comment "The reserve body has no channel — 'sms' for now"; route.ts:128-133 does the same for reserve-batch. Result: rates.ts mmsMultiplier (3x, line 35) and rcsFlatPerMessage (line 36) are unreachable from the engine reserve path — every MMS is undercharged 3x and RCS is priced per-segment-by-country (e.g. 2 credits to non-IN/US/CA instead of flat 1). The e2e only exercises 1-segment US SMS so this is masked.
gaps.fixSummary: In src/app/api/sabsms/credits/route.ts pass the body's channel through: `channel: (body.channel === 'mms' || body.channel === 'rcs' ? body.channel : 'sms')` in both the op=reserve (line ~82) and op=reserve-batch (line ~128) branches; add `channel?: string` to ReserveBatchBody if campaigns ever send it; delete the stale comment. Add a vitest in src/lib/sabsms/__tests__ asserting mms=3x and rcs=flat through the route-level pricing call.

gaps.title: Finalise true-up mixes provider cost (cents) with credits — Telnyx/Twilio real sends mis-adjust the balance
gaps.detail: worker.rs:822-831 finalises with `actual_cost: r.cost.unwrap_or(0)`. Adapter `cost` is PROVIDER cost in cents: twilio.rs:301-307 ("price as a negative dollar string... convert to positive cents"), telnyx.rs:185-186 ("cost: {\"amount\":\"0.004\"} → cents"). ledger.ts:283-297 then treats actualCost as CREDITS: when `actualCost > 0 && actualCost !== reservation.amount` it $inc's the user balance by `reservation.amount - actualCost` and logs an 'adjust' row. Example: a Telnyx international send costing 3 cents against a 1-credit hold extra-charges 2 credits; a Twilio IN send with price returned (~1-5 cents) does the same. The mock (cost=Some(1), mock.rs:68/75) also corrupts any multi-segment mock send (held 2, 'actual' 1 → bogus 1-credit refund). Masked in e2e because the only charged send is 1 segment US (held 1 == cost 1 cent).
gaps.fixSummary: Pick one unit for the finalise contract. Cleanest: engine should not forward provider cents as actualCost — in worker.rs pass actual SEGMENTS (add `actualSegments` to CreditFinaliseRequest in types.rs + src/lib/sabsms/types.ts) and have route.ts recompute the credit price via creditCostForWorkspace(actualSegments, country, channel) before calling finaliseCredits; keep provider cents in a separate field for analytics. Alternatively, stop true-up entirely (drop actualCost from the adjust path in ledger.ts) until a credits-denominated value exists.

gaps.title: Generic webhook DLR lets one tenant's account update another workspace's message
gaps.detail: On the generic route `POST /webhook/{provider}/{account_id}/{direction}`, auth binds the caller to ONE account/workspace (webhook.rs:129-147, 174-196), but the DLR branch then loads the original message by providerMessageId alone: webhook.rs:230-232 `find_one(doc! { "providerMessageId": &evt.provider_message_id })` — no `workspaceId` filter — and dlr_core mutates whatever doc matches (status flip, errorCode, suppression upsert into the VICTIM's workspace via webhook.rs:641-647, campaign stat bumps). A tenant with any valid provider account + its URL secret can forge DLRs for other tenants' messages if it can guess/observe a providerMessageId (MSG91/Gupshup ids are not high-entropy). The legacy route is safe because the signature is verified against the original message's own workspace creds (webhook.rs:545-556).
gaps.fixSummary: In handle_account's "dlr" arm (webhook.rs:225-241) scope the lookup to the authenticated workspace: `find_one(doc! { "providerMessageId": &evt.provider_message_id, "workspaceId": &workspace_id })` (optionally also `providerAccountId: &account_id`); return matched:false otherwise. Add a unit/e2e fixture asserting a cross-workspace DLR is ignored.

gaps.title: Reservation expiry sweep only fires on credits-route traffic — no scheduled job
gaps.detail: releaseExpiredHolds is invoked exactly once in the codebase: route.ts:51 `void releaseExpiredHolds().catch(() => {})` inside POST /api/sabsms/credits, capped at 20 holds per call (ledger.ts:306). So expired 15-min holds (engine crash between reserve and finalise) are refunded only when the NEXT engine credits callback of any workspace arrives; on an idle deployment, debited balances sit frozen indefinitely, and a mass-crash backlog drains at 20 per send. The sweep itself works (verified logic ledger.ts:306-339), so this is a deferral, not fake — but the plan's "TTL auto-release" implies time-driven release. Also note reserveInternal debits the balance BEFORE inserting the reservation doc (ledger.ts:183-204); a crash in that window leaks credits the sweep can never find.
gaps.fixSummary: Add a periodic caller: the existing PM2 events worker (scripts/sabsms-events-worker.mjs) is the natural host — setInterval(60s) calling releaseExpiredHolds(200) via a small exported helper, or a dedicated scripts/sabsms-credits-sweeper.mjs PM2 app. Optionally reverse the reserve order (insert reservation doc first with status 'pending', then debit, then flip to 'held') to close the leak window.

gaps.title: e2e script does not cover three of the four V2.0 acceptance criteria end-to-end

But against the plan's accept list: (1) "provider kill → retry → dead-letter" — only asserts attempts>=1 and explicitly skips the terminal max_retries state (line 264 note); no cargo test covers it either (tests/ has only dlt_corpus.rs + segment_vectors.rs). (2) "workspace-A creds never leak to B" — covered only by creds.rs unit tests (tampered_aad_fails), no black-box assertion. (3) No coverage of op=reserve-batch, hold TTL release, or the retry-path credit release (balance restored after [RETRY]).

gaps.title: Mock provider is selectable in production via the message doc's provider field
gaps.detail: registry.rs:34 returns the Mock adapter unconditionally, and worker.rs select_provider (line 67-72) accepts any registry-backed provider from the doc field — so a message enqueued with provider:"mock" "sends" (and CHARGES credits) without SABSMS_PROVIDER_MOCK=true. Webhook routes do gate mock on the env flag (webhook.rs:60-64, 83), but the send path doesn't. Exposure is limited (enqueue requires the service token; the user-facing send action doesn't pass provider), but any future Next code path that forwards a caller-supplied provider string becomes a fake-send/credit-burn hole.
gaps.fixSummary: Gate the worker the same way as webhooks: in select_provider (worker.rs:67-72) return None for ProviderId::Mock unless SABSMS_PROVIDER_MOCK=true (mirror parse_provider in webhook.rs:60-64), or make registry::provider(Mock) None outside mock mode and special-case the env override.

gaps.title: Segment counter misbills real carrier traffic on BOTH sides — and the anti-drift fixture pins the wrong math
gaps.detail: CONFIRMED, and it flows into actual credit debits. (1) GSM-7 extension chars counted as 1 septet instead of 2: providers/mod.rs:209-223 counts chars().count(); mod.rs:251 puts '€ [ ] { } \ ~ ^ |' in the single-char set; segments.ts:50 + 77-83 mirror it; comments on both sides admit it (mod.rs:202-208 'real GSM packing bills them as 2 septets', segments.ts:16-18 'close-enough for billing'). Real impact: fixture vector '160 euro signs … 1 segment' — a carrier packs 160×€ as 320 septets = 3 billed segments (ceil(320/153)); platform charges 1, eats 2 segments of provider cost — 3x under-bill. (2) UCS-2 counted in Unicode code points, not UTF-16 code units: fixture '69 ascii + emoji = 70 code points — 1 segment' is really 71 UTF-16 units = 2 carrier segments (2x under-bill); '72 astral emoji — 2 segments' is really 144 units = 3 segments (33% under-bill). Every emoji marketing blast is systematically under-billed. (3) Bonus misclassification the suspect list missed: is_gsm7_char (mod.rs:241-252) and GSM7_SINGLE_CHARS (segments.ts:42-51) OMIT GSM 03.38 basic-set chars ¿, ¤, and Greek capitals Δ Φ Γ Λ Ω Π Ψ Σ Θ Ξ — the fixture even enshrines '¿Hola?' as UCS-2 ('inverted question mark is NOT in the engine set'). Carriers bill ¿ as GSM-7, so a 160-char Spanish promo with ¿ is billed ceil(160/67)=3 segments vs carrier 1 — customers OVER-charged 3x. Billing path proof: handlers/send.rs:70 stores estimate_segments at enqueue → worker.rs:299-303 reads segmentsCount (or recomputes) → worker.rs:545-556 credit reserve with that count → src/app/api/sabsms/credits/route.ts:82-83 charges creditCostForWorkspace({segments}). Twilio's true num_segments only lands AFTER charge (twilio.rs:299 → worker.rs:804 segmentsCount write-back) and credits::finalise (worker.rs:822-832) reconciles cost, not segments. The 27-vector fixture makes both test suites GREEN while the math is wrong — the anti-drift artifact certifies the bug.
gaps.fixSummary: Coordinated 3-file fix: (a) services/sabsms-engine/src/providers/mod.rs — rewrite estimate_segments to septet-accurate GSM packing (extension chars = 2 septets via ESC, split at 160 septets / 153 with UDH) and UCS-2 sized in UTF-16 code units (70/67); add ¿ ¤ and Greek caps to is_gsm7_char; (b) src/lib/sabsms/segments.ts — identical math (use body.length-style UTF-16 counting for UCS-2, septet weights for GSM); (c) regenerate segment-vectors.json with corrected expectations + new vectors for ESC-char boundary (e.g. 80×'[' = 1 seg, 81×'[' = 2) and emoji UTF-16 boundaries; update the 'inverted question mark' vector to gsm7. Re-check composer.tsx perSegment copy and __tests__/segments.test.ts hardcoded asserts (lines 58-71 assert code-point semantics — must flip).

gaps.title: Template approval workflow enforces NOTHING — draft/rejected marketing templates send freely from every path
gaps.detail: The draft→submitted→approved lifecycle exists in the UI (approvals queue persists decisions at approvals/actions.ts:274-289, 437-440, 523) but is consulted by NOTHING on any send path: (1) sendSmsAction loads the template by _id + workspaceId only — no status filter (send/actions.ts:111-115) — so a rejected marketing template sends fine; (2) listSendableTemplates deliberately offers draft + submitted templates to the composer (send/actions.ts:278); (3) the campaign template picker also includes drafts (campaigns/new/page.tsx:89 `status: { $in: ["approved", "draft"] }`); (4) the engine compliance kernel pre_send_checks (compliance/mod.rs:198+) covers suppression/quiet-hours/consent/10DLC/DLT-registry but never looks up the workspace SMS template's status — the only 'approved' fields in the engine are credit-reservation responses (worker.rs:555, campaigns.rs:474), unrelated. Additionally reviewer == submitter == workspace owner (approvals/actions.ts:40-48), so 'approval' is self-attested. Net: the approvals page, KPI cards ('Pending approval'), SLA timers, and AI verdicts are decorative.
gaps.fixSummary: Enforce at three layers: (a) send/actions.ts sendSmsAction — when category is 'marketing' (or template.category is marketing) require templateId AND doc.status === 'approved', else return a typed error; filter listSendableTemplates to approved-only for marketing category; (b) campaigns/new/page.tsx:89 — drop 'draft' from the marketing template query; (c) engine backstop in compliance::pre_send_checks — when ctx.template_id is set and category is Marketing, look up sabsms_templates status and Block with a 'template_approval' TraceEntry if not approved (mirrors the existing DLT-registry pattern in dlt_store.rs).

gaps.title: Cross-tenant IDOR: loadTemplates / getTemplateKpis / getAvailableTags are server actions that trust a caller-supplied workspaceId
gaps.detail: templates/actions.ts is a 'use server' module (line 1), so EVERY exported async function is a POST-able server-action endpoint. loadTemplates(workspaceId, filters) (line 62), getTemplateKpis(workspaceId) (line 123), and getAvailableTags(workspaceId) (line 142) take workspaceId as a plain parameter and never call resolveWorkspace() — unlike every mutation in the same file (e.g. duplicateTemplate line 160). An authenticated user who knows/guesses another user's Mongo _id (workspaceId == user _id per resolveWorkspace at line 35; ObjectIds embed timestamps and leak throughout SabNode) can read another tenant's full template bodies, DLT entity/template IDs, and usage KPIs. Contrast with the approvals module which clamps the same pattern correctly (approvals/actions.ts:167-173 honours filters.workspaceId only when it equals the session workspace).
gaps.fixSummary: Make loadTemplates/getTemplateKpis/getAvailableTags resolve the workspace internally via resolveWorkspace() and drop the workspaceId parameter (callers: templates/page.tsx:51-53 already has the session — simplest is to keep the param for the RSC call path but move these three reads into a non-'use server' module imported only by the server page, OR add the approvals-style clamp). Audit other 'use server' files for the same pattern.

gaps.title: Engine consent-log writes use event/method fields — invisible to the canonical kind/captureMethod schema the consent page and all Next writers use
gaps.detail: keywords.rs:186-195 (STOP) writes {event:'opt_out', method:'sms_keyword', phone,...} and keywords.rs:239-248 (START) writes {event:'opt_in_restored',...} into sabsms_consent_log. The canonical SabsmsConsentEvent (types.ts:389-402) has kind:SabsmsConsentKind + captureMethod (incl. 'inbound_keyword'), and EVERY Next writer uses it (inbox/actions.ts:711, suppressions/actions.ts:373/439/518, consent/actions.ts:504, agent/handlers.ts:149). Consequences: (1) consent page filters/projections key on kind+captureMethod (consent/actions.ts:151-156, 109-124) so engine keyword opt-outs render with blank kind and are excluded from kind filters; (2) TCPA jurisdiction badge counts kind:'opt_out_stop' (consent/actions.ts:220, 238-239) → always reports 0 'STOP keyword captures' even when STOPs flow — the compliance ledger misreports the exact V2.2 acceptance event; (3) conversely the engine marketing consent gate reads only the 'event' field (compliance/mod.rs:278, CONSENT_GRANTING_EVENTS at :113 = opt_in/opt_in_restored/double_opt_in) so Next-written opt-ins (kind:'opt_in_single'/'opt_in_double'/'opt_in_restart') never grant consent. Two writers, two readers, two disjoint schemas on one collection.
gaps.fixSummary: Pick the TS schema (V2.12's guardrail comment at agent/handlers.ts:137-139 already declares it canonical). In keywords.rs change consent-log inserts to kind:'opt_out_stop' / kind:'opt_in_restart' + captureMethod:'inbound_keyword'; update compliance/mod.rs consent gate to read 'kind' with granting values ['opt_in_single','opt_in_double','opt_in_restart'] (keep reading 'event' as fallback during migration); add a one-shot backfill script mapping event→kind on existing docs. Update keywords.rs/compliance tests accordingly.

gaps.title: Engine suppression docs swap source/reason vs the canonical schema — 'STOP keyword' facet misses all keyword suppressions; START cannot clear schema-conformant ones
gaps.detail: keywords.rs:169-175 writes suppressions as {reason:'stop', source:'keyword'} but SabsmsSuppressionSource (types.ts) is 'stop'|'complaint'|'bounce'|'manual'|'carrier_block'|'import' — 'keyword' is not a legal source, and the V2.12 guardrail explicitly writes the schema-correct swap (agent/handlers.ts:137-145: "the suppression schema enum owns source, so source='stop' + reason='ai_intent'"). The suppressions page source facet maps 'STOP keyword' → value 'stop' (suppressions-table.tsx:86-93, 464-471), so filtering by 'STOP keyword' returns ZERO engine-created keyword suppressions, and their badge renders the off-enum value 'keyword' (suppressions-table.tsx:175). Additionally the engine START handler deletes only {reason:'stop'} (keywords.rs:227-233), so AI-guardrail suppressions (source:'stop', reason:'ai_intent') are never cleared when a customer texts START — they stay suppressed after opting back in.
gaps.fixSummary: In keywords.rs write {source:'stop', reason:'keyword:'+normalized} and change the START delete filter to {source:'stop'} (covers both keyword and AI-guardrail STOPs); add a migration/backfill for existing {source:'keyword'} docs. Optionally extend the START path to also log against docs it clears.

gaps.title: Per-workspace custom keywords (sabsms_keyword_rules) have no management UI or server action anywhere

Also keywords.rs:100-101 merges only stopKeywords + helpKeywords; startKeywords is never merged despite the struct field (keywords.rs:43), so custom START keywords are silently ignored even if the doc existed.

Add the missing merge_extras("startKeywords") line in keywords.rs.

gaps.title: Inbox reply attachments are silently dropped — engine sends MMS only from mediaUrls, which no sabsms caller ever sets
gaps.detail: replyToThread passes media: [{sabFileId, mime:'application/octet-stream', bytes:0}] (inbox/actions.ts:323-327) and the thread composer exposes SabFilePickerButton attachments with a success toast (thread-view.tsx:138, 210-227). But the engine worker builds provider SendOptions only from the 'mediaUrls' doc field (worker.rs:78-102; types.rs:158-162 — 'Resolved public URLs... pre-resolved Next-side'), and a repo-wide grep shows NO sabsms code path ever sets mediaUrls on enqueueSend. Attachments are stored as inert metadata and never delivered; the user sees 'Reply queued' and assumes the MMS went out.
gaps.fixSummary: In replyToThread resolve each sabFileId to its public R2 URL (SabFiles lib already exposes file lookup used by the picker) and pass both media (real mime/bytes) and mediaUrls to sabsmsEngine.enqueueSend. Same fix applies to any composer that later adds attachments (send page currently has none).

gaps.title: Webhook retries inflate conversation unreadCount despite the 'skip side effects' idempotency guard
gaps.detail: The conversation upsert with $inc unreadCount + lastMessageAt/preview update (webhook.rs:394-418) executes BEFORE the duplicate-key-checked message insert (webhook.rs:456-499). On a provider webhook retry the insert is a no-op ('duplicate inbound webhook delivery; skipping side effects', :498) but the unread counter was already bumped again — drift between unreadCount and actual unseen messages until the next markRead.
gaps.fixSummary: Reorder: insert the message first (without conversationId), then on non-duplicate do the conversation upsert and $set conversationId on the message — or move the $inc into the non-duplicate branch with a second targeted update_one.

gaps.title: The promoted 'Advanced Builder' wizard (/sabsms/campaigns/new) launches campaigns that send NOTHING
gaps.detail: new/actions.ts launchCampaign (lines 118-200) just flips the campaign doc to status 'running'/'scheduled' in Mongo — it inserts ZERO docs into sabsms_campaign_recipients and never calls the engine. The TODO at new/actions.ts:187-191 says 'Phase 4: enqueue per-recipient send jobs... the engine will pick the campaign up via a Mongo change-stream watcher' — referencing the OLD plan (plans/sabsms-world-class-plan.md); no change-stream watcher exists. The V2.3 engine ticker then finds the 'running' campaign, claims nothing (campaigns.rs:432-443), counts 0 pending/claimed (campaigns.rs:446-456), and marks it 'completed' within ~2s — the user sees a 'completed' campaign with zero messages sent. This broken wizard is the PROMOTED path: SabSMS dashboard quick action (src/app/sabsms/page.tsx:60), campaigns empty state (campaigns-table.tsx:574), quick-send upsell copy (quick-send/page.tsx:35-56, quick-send-client.tsx:264-265), and ab-tests page (ab-tests/page.tsx:59) all link /sabsms/campaigns/new. The actually-working path (createCampaignAction + launchCampaignAction in campaigns/actions.ts:784/1084, which pre-renders recipients and calls sabsmsEngine.launchCampaign) is only reached via /sabsms/campaigns/create (campaigns/page.tsx:95).
gaps.fixSummary: Rewire new/actions.ts launchCampaign/scheduleCampaign to delegate to the real path: map CampaignDraft → createCampaignAction input, then call launchCampaignAction (both in src/app/sabsms/campaigns/actions.ts), deleting the status-only flip and the stale Phase-4 TODO. Alternatively delete /campaigns/new and repoint the 5 inbound links to /campaigns/create. Also add a defensive guard in services/sabsms-engine/src/campaigns.rs launch()/promote_scheduled(): refuse to flip a campaign to running when count_documents({campaignId, status:'pending'}) == 0, returning a 'no recipients staged' error.

gaps.title: Engine ticker ignores notBeforeEpochMs — V2.10 smart-send per-recipient windows are inert data
gaps.detail: Confirmed: launchCampaignAction stamps notBeforeEpochMs per recipient from the identity graph (campaigns/actions.ts:1137-1149, 1255-1267 stampSmartSendWindows), but grep of services/sabsms-engine/src/ for notBeforeEpochMs/not_before returns ZERO hits. The claim filter at campaigns.rs:434-437 is exactly {campaignId, status:'pending'} — recipients with a future notBefore are claimed and sent immediately. The Next side documents the gap itself (actions.ts:1138-1142: 'The engine ticker does NOT read this field yet'), so only the campaign-level median scheduledAt actually shifts delivery; per-contact best-hour precision — the V2.10 smart-send differentiator — is dead end-to-end.
gaps.fixSummary: In campaigns.rs process_campaign, extend the claim filter with $or:[{notBeforeEpochMs:{$exists:false}},{notBeforeEpochMs:{$lte: now_ms}}] (and add {campaignId, status, notBeforeEpochMs} to the recipient indexes in db.rs). Alternative per the plan: claim normally but route future-notBefore recipients through delayed::schedule(conn, &message_id, not_before_epoch_secs) after materialising the message doc, so the existing 1s delayed ZSET ticker (delayed.rs:17-24) releases them. Add a cargo unit test for the filter predicate.

gaps.title: Campaign DETAIL page pause/resume/cancel bypass the engine — cancel leaves recipients pending, no transition guards, no events
gaps.detail: [id]/actions.ts:660-681 implements pauseCampaign/resumeCampaign/cancelCampaign as a raw cols.campaigns.updateOne({$set:{status:next}}) with NO status precondition and NO engine call; campaign-detail-client.tsx:39-51 imports these (lines 296/309/322 invoke them). Contrast: the engine's cancel also marks all pending|claimed recipients 'cancelled' (campaigns.rs:313-323) and every transition is status-guarded + emits CampaignPaused/Started events (campaigns.rs:232-290). Consequences of the bypass: (1) cancel from the detail page leaves recipients status 'pending' forever — any later flip back to 'running' (resumeCampaign has no guard, so it can 'resume' a cancelled/completed campaign) restarts sending; (2) statusReason ('insufficient_credits') is never cleared on resume; (3) the events stream consumers never see the pause/cancel. The LIST table uses the correct engine-backed versions (campaigns/actions.ts:401-423 → sabsmsEngine.pause/resume/cancelCampaign with an engine-disabled dev fallback) — so the same buttons behave differently on the two pages. Note the list page's own header comment (campaigns/page.tsx:5-8) claims ALL mutations route through the Rust engine.
gaps.fixSummary: Delete the three direct-Mongo implementations in src/app/sabsms/campaigns/[id]/actions.ts (lines ~650-681) and re-export the engine-backed pauseCampaign/resumeCampaign/cancelCampaign from ../actions; update campaign-detail-client.tsx imports. Keep setStatus only as the engineDisabled fallback already provided by engineStatusAction in ../actions.

gaps.title: Campaigns cannot carry MMS media or RCS payloads — single-send parity gap, while the wizard shows an RCS-capable estimate
gaps.detail: Confirmed: CampaignRecipientDoc (launch-helpers.ts:104-118) carries only a pre-rendered text body — no mediaUrls, no rcs payload, no channelRequested. The engine's enqueue_recipient (campaigns.rs:646-661) hardcodes channel:'sms' and copies nothing else, so campaign messages can never be MMS or rcs_preferred. Meanwhile the single-send pipeline fully supports both: worker.rs:78-102 reads mediaUrls off the message doc into SendOptions, worker.rs:501-514 implements rcs_preferred channel selection, and SabsmsEnqueueSendInput exposes mediaUrls/rcs/channelRequested (lib/sabsms/types.ts:494-504). The working create wizard even fetches and displays '~N% RCS-capable' for the audience (create/page.tsx:86-149, 635-643) while offering no way to attach a rich card or media — V2.11's 'one campaign → rich card on RCS Android, SMS fallback' acceptance is impossible through campaigns. Also: recipients support providerAccountId (launch-helpers.ts:111) but launchCampaignAction never sets it, and campaigns.rs:655 pins provider:'twilio', so non-Twilio workspaces without a routing policy can't campaign at all.
gaps.fixSummary: Extend CampaignRecipientDoc + CreateCampaignSchema with mediaUrls?/rcs?/channelRequested? (campaign-level, copied per recipient in buildRecipientDocs); in campaigns.rs enqueue_recipient, copy mediaUrls/rcs/channelRequested from the campaign doc onto the message doc (the worker already consumes them) and stop hardcoding channel. Surface SabFilePicker media + rich-card composer in the create wizard (reuse the send-page composer). Pass campaign.providerAccountId through to recipients.

gaps.title: The only working wizard supports segment audiences exclusively — list/CSV audiences unreachable

Remove or wire the dead Smart Routing / A/B switches and expose throttlePerSec (the schema already accepts it).

Add node:test coverage for launch-helpers (buildRecipientDocs idempotency keys, dedupeContacts, parseCsv). Optionally a mongodb-backed #[ignore] cargo test for concurrent claim safety.

gaps.title: 'Token bucket' throttle is actually a fixed per-tick claim quota (2s bursts, no carryover)
gaps.detail: Spec says 'token-bucket throttle'. Implementation is batch_quota = clamp(throttlePerSec,1,500) × TICK_SECS claimed every 2s tick (campaigns.rs:84-101, 423, 432) and LPUSHed immediately — i.e. bursts of 2× throttle every 2s with no refill smoothing or unused-capacity carryover, and actual wire pacing is then governed by worker throughput, not the campaign throttle. Average rate ≈ throttle, so the '5k @ 10 msg/s' acceptance roughly holds; the module docs (campaigns.rs:17-29) document the per-doc claim design but not the bucket deviation.
gaps.fixSummary: Acceptable deferral if documented; for true bucket semantics keep a per-campaign Redis token counter (refill throttlePerSec/s, claim up to available tokens per tick) in campaigns.rs process_campaign, ~60 LOC + tests. At minimum amend the module doc and the plan to say 'per-tick quota approximating a token bucket'.

gaps.title: Batch credit gate prices the whole batch country-blind (destinationCountry always '')
gaps.detail: The ticker's CampaignBatchReserveRequest (campaigns.rs:465-472) sends estimated_cost:0 and no destinationCountry; the Next callback prices segmentsTotal at destinationCountry '' (credits/route.ts:126-133 `body.destinationCountry ?? ''`), i.e. the default rate. For IN/US-heavy batches whose per-segment rates differ from the default, the affordability gate under/over-estimates — a campaign can pass the gate then stall mid-flight on per-message holds (it pauses correctly via insufficient_credits, so billing stays correct; only the gate's accuracy suffers).
gaps.fixSummary: Have process_campaign group the claimed batch by countryFromE164-equivalent (or pass a byCountry segment map) in CampaignBatchReserveRequest, and price per country in the reserve-batch op (creditCostForWorkspace already takes destinationCountry). ~30 LOC across campaigns.rs/types.rs/route.ts.

gaps.title: MMS send path is dead end-to-end on the Next side — media silently dropped
gaps.detail: The engine only ever attaches media from the doc field `mediaUrls` (worker.rs:78 `get_array("mediaUrls")` → SendOptions.media_urls → twilio.rs:236-237 MediaUrl params), and EnqueueSendInput.mediaUrls exists in both contracts (engine types.rs:162, Next types.ts:494). But NOTHING Next-side ever populates it: grep for `mediaUrls` across src/ hits only type/zod declarations (types.ts:217,494; db/collections.ts:365) — zero call sites. The public API (api/v1/sms/messages/route.ts:171-175) and inbox reply (inbox/actions.ts:323-327) accept `mediaSabFileIds` and pass only `media: [{sabFileId, mime:'application/octet-stream', bytes:0}]` metadata — the engine never resolves sabFileId→URL (grep `sab_file_id` in engine = types.rs:125 declaration only), so these sends go out as plain SMS with attachments silently dropped, while the OpenAPI spec (apikeys/openapi.ts:149) advertises 'SabFiles file ids for MMS media' as working. The quick-send composer has NO MMS attachment UI at all — its only SabFilePickerButton (composer.tsx:835) is the RCS card image inside `rcsMode`, and SendSmsInput (send/actions.ts:64-86) has no media field. Inbound MMS: webhook.rs:439-440 stores provider `mediaUrls` on inbound docs, but the inbox projection maps `doc.media` (absent on inbound) → mediaIds (inbox/actions.ts:123), so inbound MMS images never render (thread-view.tsx:1050-1057 shows only truncated-id badges anyway), and there is no download-to-SabFiles of inbound media (Twilio media URLs expire/require auth).
gaps.fixSummary: 1) Add a SabFiles id→public-R2-URL resolver (reuse the share/public URL helper from @/components/sabfiles backend) and call it in api/v1/sms/messages/route.ts and inbox/actions.ts replyToThread to populate `mediaUrls` (and set channel:'mms') alongside the existing `media` metadata. 2) Add an MMS attachments section to send/composer.tsx using SabFilePickerButton (multi, image/video), thread `mediaUrls` through SendSmsInput → sabsmsEngine.enqueueSend in send/actions.ts. 3) For inbound: project `doc.mediaUrls` into InboxMessageView and render previews in thread-view.tsx; optionally add a worker/consumer job that downloads inbound provider media into SabFiles per the SabFiles policy.

gaps.title: Compliance dashboard page is a 100% hardcoded mock presented as wired
gaps.detail: Plan V2.4A says 'wire compliance page (319) ... to real state'. The page is still exactly 319 lines of static mock: fabricated stats ('EU Consent Coverage 89%', 'TRAI Adherence 99.9%' — compliance/page.tsx:38-57), fake registry status ('Brand: Approved, 2 Active Campaigns', 'PEID: Active, 4 Headers, 12 Templates' :67-88), a fake compliance officer 'Alice Liddell' with a pravatar.cc avatar (:184-191), fake unsubscribe timeline phone numbers (:251,261), export actions that are `console.log("Exporting PDF")` (:19-26), and quiet-hours copy that contradicts the engine ('21:00 - 09:00 IST blocked' at :283-285 — the engine window is 10:00–21:00 allowed, i.e. blocked 21:00–10:00). Every number a user sees on this governance page is fake.
gaps.fixSummary: Convert to a server component (or client + server actions): consent coverage from aggregates over sabsms_consent_log/suppressions/contacts; registry status from sabsms_provider_accounts.tenDlc + the DLT registry collections; quiet-hours card generated from the engine WINDOWS table constants (correct 10:00–21:00 IST copy); recent STOP events from inbound messages/consent log; real CSV export action. Delete the fake officer/webhook cards or back them with sabsms_settings.

gaps.title: 10DLC page mocks TCR registration, and tenDlc.status is unsettable anywhere — US marketing permanently blocked on real accounts
gaps.detail: The engine kernel correctly blocks US marketing unless the provider account doc has `tenDlc.status == "verified"` (compliance/mod.rs:127-142, 300-341). But grep for `tenDlc` across ALL of src/ returns ZERO hits, and across engine handlers/*.rs zero hits — no UI, server action, or engine endpoint can ever set it; only a manual Mongo edit unblocks US marketing. Meanwhile 10dlc/actions.ts:3 is literally commented '// Mocking direct API integration with The Campaign Registry (TCR)': fetchTcrStatus returns hardcoded trust score 95/12 brands after a fake 800ms delay (:5-21), and registerBrand/registerCampaign return success with `B-${Math.floor(Math.random()*1000000)}` ids (:23-46) — a user who 'registers' a brand here gets a success toast and nothing persists. The page's primaryAction is `onClick: () => {}` (10dlc/page.tsx:39-41). The plan required 'wire ... 10dlc page to real state' with manual tenDlc entry on provider accounts.
gaps.fixSummary: 1) Add a tenDlc subdocument editor (status: none|pending|verified, brandId, campaignId, notes) to the provider-account detail UI (providers/[id]) writing to sabsms_provider_accounts via an authenticated server action (workspace-scoped). 2) Replace 10dlc/actions.ts mocks with real reads of provider accounts + their tenDlc state and persist registrations to a sabsms_tendlc_registrations collection (manual-entry workflow; real TCR API integration can stay deferred but must not fake success). 3) Surface the kernel's ten_dlc block code in the page so users see why sends were rejected.

gaps.title: Branded short-link domain mints dead URLs — no host-based serving path exists
gaps.detail: Saving a branded domain (settings/short-links-card.tsx) makes links-core.ts:126-128 mint links as `https://{domain}/{slug}` (root path, no /s prefix), and the card's copy claims the domain 'serves /<slug> through the /s redirect'. But nothing serves it: (a) proxy.ts's matcher (:153-164) only runs on /dashboard, /wachat, /admin/dashboard and auth pages — arbitrary root paths on a foreign host never reach the custom-domain rewrite at :81-85; (b) even if it did, /_domain/[host]/[[...path]]/page.tsx resolves ONLY sabflow_custom_domains flows (file header comment + getDomainByName); (c) the root catch-all src/app/[shortCode]/page.tsx queries only the legacy `short_urls` collection (:15) — zero references to sabsms/getShortLinkBySlug (grep confirmed). So every link minted under a branded domain 404s for recipients: clicks are lost AND the destination never loads. Only the default `${APP_URL}/s/{slug}` base works (via /s/[shortCode]/page.tsx).
gaps.fixSummary: Add a SabSMS first-look lookup to the root catch-all src/app/[shortCode]/page.tsx mirroring s/[shortCode]/page.tsx:52-66 (getShortLinkBySlug + recordClick + redirect), optionally validating the request Host against sabsms_settings.shortLinkDomain for tenant isolation. Alternatively mint branded links as https://{domain}/s/{slug} and extend the proxy matcher — but the root-dispatcher fix is smaller and matches the existing dual-dispatcher pattern.

gaps.title: Campaign 'Link Tracking' toggle is decorative — campaign sends never shorten links or attribute clicks
gaps.detail: campaigns/create/page.tsx:532-537 shows a 'Link Tracking — Automatically shorten URLs and track click-through rates' card with `<Switch defaultChecked />` — the switch is bound to no state, never serialized to the campaign doc, and grep for shorten/createShortLink across campaigns/actions.ts, launch-helpers.ts, helpers.ts and engine campaigns.rs returns zero hits. Campaign messages (the primary marketing volume, where per-contact click attribution matters most — createShortLink even supports contactId for exactly this) go out with raw long URLs while the UI implies tracking is ON by default. The shortener is only reachable from the single-send composer (off by default) and journeys.
gaps.fixSummary: Bind the toggle to campaign create state and persist `shortenLinks` on the campaign doc; in the recipient pre-render path (campaigns launch/recipient chunk build in launch-helpers.ts/actions.ts) call shortenUrlsInBody per recipient with {workspaceId, campaignId, contactId, baseOverride: pre-resolved base} so each contact gets an attributed slug, before recipients are written to sabsms_campaign_recipients.

gaps.title: complianceTrace has no structured rendering in logs detail — only the raw-JSON dump
gaps.detail: Plan: '`complianceTrace` in logs detail'. The engine persists the trace on every verdict (worker.rs:388-450) and logs/page.tsx fetches full docs (:61-65) so the trace IS technically visible inside the collapsed 'Raw document' <details> JSON dump (:113 rawJson, :376-385) — but the message-detail dialog renders dedicated sections for timeline/error/attempts (:318-374) and nothing for complianceTrace, so the headline V2.4 feature (e.g. quiet_hours → reschedule with the until timestamp, consent/ten_dlc verdicts) is invisible unless a user expands raw JSON.
gaps.fixSummary: Add a 'Compliance' section to the detail dialog in logs/page.tsx mapping the complianceTrace array to check/verdict/detail rows (badge per verdict: allow/skipped/warn/block/reschedule), plus rescheduledUntil/rescheduleCode when present.

gaps.title: HEAD/bot requests are counted as clicks (known V2.4B caveat)
gaps.detail: s/[shortCode]/page.tsx:50-51 documents it: 'bots redirect AND count (UA is stored — filtering happens in analytics later)'. recordClick (:56-64) runs for every render, including HEAD prefetches and link-preview crawlers (SMS apps fetch previews aggressively, so click counts will be inflated immediately after send). UA is persisted on the click doc so retroactive filtering is possible.
gaps.fixSummary: Short-term: skip recordClick for known bot/preview UAs (a small denylist incl. facebookexternalhit, WhatsApp, Twitterbot, Slackbot, HeadlessChrome) and for HEAD via request method when available; long-term filter in V2.10 analytics rollups using the stored userAgent.

gaps.title: SABSMS_REQUIRE_CONSENT undocumented in .env.example; stray scratch files inside the logs route
gaps.detail: .env.example documents SABSMS_ALLOW_ENV_CREDS (:44) and SABSMS_SHORT_LINK_BASE (:53) but not SABSMS_REQUIRE_CONSENT (read at compliance/mod.rs:146-150, default true) — an operator can't discover the consent-gating switch. Also src/app/sabsms/logs/ contains leftover dev junk: scratch.ts (2 lines, '// testing code') and test_cursor.ts (1 line) checked into a route directory.
gaps.fixSummary: Add SABSMS_REQUIRE_CONSENT=true (+ comment: fail-closed marketing consent gate) to .env.example next to the other SABSMS_ vars; delete logs/scratch.ts and logs/test_cursor.ts.

verifiedOk: Quiet hours table + logic: IN marketing 10:00–21:00 IST strict, US/CA conservative dual-tz bounding windows (08:00 LA → 21:00 NY), marketing-only exemption, DST-safe next-window computation — services/sabsms-engine/src/compliance/quiet_hours.rs:42-122 with 12 table-driven cargo tests (:124-261) covering the exact plan acceptance case (22:00 IST → next 10:00 IST).

Full pre-send kernel runs in the worker BEFORE credit reservation, in order suppression → quiet hours → consent → 10DLC → DLT, and persists complianceTrace on the message doc for every verdict (Allow/Block/Reschedule) — compliance/mod.rs:198-449, worker.rs:316-470.

Reschedule via delayed ZSET works: Verdict::Reschedule → ZADD sabsms:delayed scored by epoch (delayed.rs:17-24), 1s ticker with ZREM atomicity guard promotes due ids back to the send list (delayed.rs:36-54), ticker spawned in main.rs:59; rescheduledUntil/rescheduleCode stamped on the doc and complianceRescheduled event emitted (worker.rs:433-468, events.rs:76,132).

Consent gating fail-closed: SABSMS_REQUIRE_CONSENT defaults true (mod.rs:146-150); latest sabsms_consent_log event must be opt_in/opt_in_restored/double_opt_in (mod.rs:113-117, 270-298) with unit tests (:491-502).

10DLC gating decision logic correct and fail-closed: US marketing blocked unless provider-account tenDlc.status=='verified'; missing account only passes with SABSMS_ALLOW_ENV_CREDS=true (mod.rs:127-142, 300-341; tests :505-538). (The unsettable status / mock UI is filed as a gap.)

Link shortener core: crypto-random base62 slugs with rejection sampling, mid-sentence URL extraction with trailing-punctuation strip, longest-first replacement, branded-domain normalization, reuse-tuple filter, truncated-sha256 IP hashing — src/lib/sabsms/links-core.ts (168 lines, pure) with 29 node:test assertions in __tests__/links.test.ts.

createShortLink/recordClick are real Mongo paths: unique-slug dup-key retry loop (links.ts:93-114), click doc insert with campaign/contact attribution + clickCount $inc (links.ts:193-221), late messageId back-fill (links.ts:229-240).

/s/[shortCode] dispatcher gives SabSMS first look ahead of the legacy short_urls module, records the click fire-and-forget (never blocks the redirect), and 302s to the target — src/app/s/[shortCode]/page.tsx:44-66.

Click events reach the event stream: emitLinkClickedEvent XADDs a linkClicked pseudo-event in the engine wire shape (events/emit.ts), consumed by the identity graph (identity/handlers.ts:25, graph.ts:204), journey triggers (journeys/triggers.ts:349), and mapped to the public webhook event link.clicked (webhooks-out/events.ts:23).

Composer auto-shorten is wired end-to-end: 'Shorten links' switch (composer.tsx:325,790-800) → sendSmsAction shortens the body pre-enqueue, fails loudly on shortener errors, estimates segments on the shortened text, and back-fills messageId attribution (send/actions.ts:151-170, 211-227).

Branded-domain settings round-trip works at the data layer: short-links-card.tsx + settings/actions.ts save/clear sabsms_settings.shortLinkDomain with hostname normalization, and resolveWorkspaceShortLinkBase/resolveShortLinkBase honor workspace → SABSMS_SHORT_LINK_BASE → APP_URL/s priority (links.ts:39-48, links-core.ts:104-134). (Serving the branded host is the filed gap.)

Engine-side MMS plumbing is genuinely complete: EnqueueSendInput.media_urls → message doc (send.rs:82) → SendOptions.media_urls (worker.rs:78-102, tests :1116-1127) → repeated Twilio MediaUrl form params (twilio.rs:235-237) with a wiremock happy-path test (:515); inbound Twilio MMS MediaUrl0..N parsed (twilio.rs:380-392) and stored as mediaUrls on inbound docs (webhook.rs:439-440). The dead Next-side feed is the filed critical gap.

Delayed-rescheduled messages re-enter the FULL kernel on promotion (status stays queued, promotion re-pushes through the same QUEUE_SEND consumer), so consent/suppression are re-checked at the new send time — worker.rs:442 comment + delayed.rs:49.

gaps.title: Numbers page 'Add number' CTA routes to the OLD MOCK wizard; the real engine-backed buy UI is orphaned
gaps.detail: numbers-client.tsx:256 — the only purchase CTA does router.push("/sabsms/numbers/new"). numbers/new/actions.ts:71-74 'searches' via generateMockAvailableNumbers (comment: "TODO(engine): replace with sabsmsEngine.searchAvailable") and provisionNumbers (lines 79-130) inserts sabsms_numbers docs DIRECTLY into Mongo with status pending — no provider purchase ever happens, so users 'buy' numbers that don't exist at Twilio/Telnyx and may try to send from them. Meanwhile the genuine V2.5 deliverable /sabsms/numbers/buy (engine search/provision via buy/actions.ts) is linked from NOWHERE: repo-wide grep for "numbers/buy" outside its own folder returns zero hits; the sabsms sidebar (app-sidebars.tsx:1498) only links /sabsms/numbers.
gaps.fixSummary: Point the CTA (numbers-client.tsx:256) and any 'Add number' affordances at /sabsms/numbers/buy; convert /sabsms/numbers/new into a redirect (same pattern as numbers/pool/page.tsx) or rebuild it on searchAvailableNumbersAction/provisionNumberAction; delete generateMockAvailableNumbers + the direct insertMany path and its wizard.test.ts mock assertions.

gaps.title: Fake 'Sync with Provider' and dead 'Update routing/webhooks' bulk actions toast success while doing nothing real
gaps.detail: numbers/actions.ts:8-34 syncNumbersWithProvider — comment literally says "For the sake of the exercise, let's simulate"; it makes no provider call and force-sets status:"active" on every selected number (line 30), which would resurrect a number in 'releasing' state. numbers/actions.ts:36-51 bulkUpdateNumbersConfig writes webhookUrl/routingUrl fields that nothing consumes — engine grep shows zero reads of those fields (engine builds DLR callbacks itself in worker.rs:763-783 and routes inbound by e164 lookup). UI (numbers-client.tsx:177,196) toasts "Successfully synced"/"Successfully updated routing/webhooks" — fake presented as working, with window.prompt free-text URL entry to boot.
gaps.fixSummary: Either remove both row/bulk actions from numbers-client.tsx, or implement a real engine sync endpoint (Twilio GET IncomingPhoneNumbers/{sid}, Telnyx GET /v2/phone_numbers/{id}) in services/sabsms-engine/src/handlers/numbers.rs + engine-client method, reflecting true provider status (including released/ported-away) instead of hardcoding active. Drop the dead webhookUrl/routingUrl writes.

gaps.title: Number release is Mongo-status-only: nothing ever releases at the provider and no job consumes scheduledReleaseAt
gaps.detail: numbers/[id]/actions.ts releaseNumber sets status:"releasing" + scheduledReleaseAt and returns ok — UI presents a working release-with-grace flow. Repo-wide grep: the only writers/readers of scheduledReleaseAt are this action, its client, and the schema; no cron, no worker, no engine ticker processes it, and the engine has no release/deprovision endpoint at all (handlers/numbers.rs only has search+provision; handlers/mod.rs routes confirm). Result: the customer keeps paying Twilio/Telnyx rent forever for a number the UI claims is being released, and the doc stays in 'releasing' limbo.
gaps.fixSummary: Add POST /v1/numbers/release to the engine (Twilio DELETE /2010-04-01/Accounts/{sid}/IncomingPhoneNumbers/{numSid}.json; Telnyx DELETE /v2/phone_numbers/{id}) + engine-client method; add a sweep for due scheduledReleaseAt docs (engine delayed.rs-style ticker or the sabsms-events PM2 worker) that calls it and flips status to released.

gaps.title: Telnyx provision stores the number-ORDER id as providerNumberId and hardcodes capabilities to sms-only
gaps.detail: numbers.rs:538-543 — after POST /v2/number_orders, parsed.data.id (the ORDER id) is persisted as providerNumberId with the comment "the order id is the durable handle". It is not: Telnyx number management (release, messaging-profile update, features) keys on the phone-number id from /v2/phone_numbers, so any future release/sync implementation (gap above) is broken from day one for Telnyx. numbers.rs:544-552 then hardcodes NumberCapabilities{sms:true,mms:false,rcs:false,voice:false}, so the numbers list/detail show false 'no MMS/voice' pills (numbers-client.tsx:121 capability pills, filters at :69-72) for every Telnyx number even when the searched number advertised mms/voice features.
gaps.fixSummary: In telnyx_provision, after the order succeeds, GET /v2/phone_numbers?filter[phone_number]={e164} to resolve the real phone-number id + features; store that id as providerNumberId (keep orderId as a separate field) and map features into capabilities. Backfill script optional.

gaps.title: Twilio number search returns hardcoded US list prices for every country
gaps.detail: numbers.rs:233-237 — monthly_cost: Some(if tollfree {215} else {115}) with comment "surface the well-known list prices". The buy UI offers US/CA/GB/AU/IN country search (buy/page.tsx:56-62) and renders this as the authoritative 'Monthly cost' column — GB/AU/CA Twilio prices differ materially, so users make purchase decisions on fabricated prices. Telnyx, by contrast, uses real cost_information (numbers.rs:307-316).
gaps.fixSummary: Call Twilio Pricing API (GET https://pricing.twilio.com/v1/PhoneNumbers/Countries/{iso}) once per search and map current_price by number_type, cached in-process; or at minimum return monthly_cost:None for non-US and label the UI value 'estimated (US list)'.

gaps.title: Monthly number-rent charging does not exist; monthlyCost never persisted at provision
gaps.detail: Repo-wide grep for rent/monthlyRent/numberRent across src/lib/sabsms, src/app/sabsms, src/app/api/sabsms and the engine: zero hits; no PM2 worker or engine ticker charges credits for held numbers. The engine's provision insert (numbers.rs:410-424) does not store monthlyCost even though search returned one, so the detail page renders monthlyCost: doc.monthlyCost ?? 0 (numbers/[id]/actions.ts loadNumberDetail) — purchased numbers show $0.00/mo and there is no recurring credit debit for them.
gaps.fixSummary: Persist monthlyCost (+currency) on the sabsms_numbers doc in provision(); add a daily/monthly rent job (extend the sabsms-events worker or an engine ticker alongside delayed.rs) that debits the credits ledger per active number via the existing /api/sabsms/credits path and suspends numbers on insufficient balance.

gaps.title: SABSMS_ENGINE_PUBLIC_URL missing from .env.example although both Next UI and engine worker read it
gaps.detail: .env.example:28-55 sabsms block documents SABSMS_ENGINE_URL, SABSMS_APP_CALLBACK_URL etc. but NOT SABSMS_ENGINE_PUBLIC_URL, which is read by webhook-urls.ts:28 (UI webhook-URL display, falls back to SABSMS_ENGINE_URL=http://localhost:4002 → providers UI shows operators localhost webhook URLs in prod) and by worker.rs:766 (per-message DLR callback auto-config is silently skipped when unset, so Telnyx/MSG91 DLR round-trips never happen without manual provider-console setup).
gaps.fixSummary: Add SABSMS_ENGINE_PUBLIC_URL= with a comment (publicly reachable engine base used for provider webhook URLs + per-message DLR callbacks) to the sabsms block of .env.example; consider a server-side warning banner in providers UI when the resolved base is localhost.

gaps.title: Legacy Twilio webhook path cannot pass signature verification: Next proxy never sends X-Forwarded-Url
gaps.detail: Engine legacy handler (webhook.rs:96-105) reconstructs the Twilio-signed URL from the x-forwarded-url header, "Next forwards the original under X-Forwarded-Url", else falls back to {app_callback_url}/webhook/twilio/{direction}. But the Next proxy (route.ts:57-74) forwards X-Sabsms-* + provider headers only — it never sets X-Forwarded-Url — and Twilio signs the URL it was configured with ({app}/api/sabsms/webhook/twilio/dlr), which never equals the fallback. So HMAC-SHA1 verification on the legacy path fails 100% of the time and DLRs/inbound get 401-dropped unless SABSMS_ALLOW_UNSIGNED_WEBHOOKS=true. The plan explicitly requires keeping the Twilio legacy route working. (The generic per-account route survives because the ?secret= fallback authenticates even when the URL-based HMAC mismatches.)
gaps.fixSummary: In route.ts add forwardHeaders['X-Forwarded-Url'] = req.url (the exact public URL Twilio signed) before fetch; add a fixture test asserting the header reaches the engine. Optionally make the engine fallback use SABSMS_ENGINE_PUBLIC_URL too.

gaps.title: Providers UI can only hold ONE account per provider per workspace (upsert keyed {workspaceId, provider})
gaps.detail: saveProviderAccountAction (actions.ts:144-163) upserts on { workspaceId, provider }, so saving 'Twilio' again silently overwrites the existing account's cipher — a second Twilio account (regional split, V2.6 same-provider failover, sender pools) is impossible from the UI even though the schema, isDefault sibling logic (actions.ts:137-141, 291-317) and engine creds::resolve(account_id) are all multi-account ready.
gaps.fixSummary: Add an explicit accountId to the save action: update by _id when editing, insertOne when creating; surface 'Add another account' for an existing provider in providers-client.tsx.

gaps.title: MSG91/Gupshup inbound idempotency hole: missing provider message id falls back to a fresh UUID
gaps.detail: msg91.rs:272-273 and gupshup.rs:427-428 default provider_message_id to format!("msg91-in-{uuid}") / "gupshup-in-{uuid}" when the callback carries no requestId/messageId (common for these providers). The inbound dedupe in webhook.rs relies on the unique providerMessageId index (webhook.rs:453-456), so provider webhook RETRIES of id-less callbacks insert duplicate messages and re-fire keyword/STOP side effects.
gaps.fixSummary: Derive a deterministic fallback id instead: hash(from|to|body|coarse timestamp bucket) in both adapters (shared helper in providers/mod.rs) so retries collide on the unique index.

gaps.title: Gupshup base-URL env var split between send adapter and test-connection probe
gaps.detail: The send adapter reads SABSMS_GUPSHUP_BASE_URL (gupshup.rs:44) while test_gupshup reads SABSMS_GUPSHUP_API_BASE (internal.rs:312); Twilio/Telnyx use one var consistently (SABSMS_TWILIO_API_BASE / SABSMS_TELNYX_API_BASE in both numbers.rs:133-141 and internal.rs:235/262). A tenant-specific Gupshup endpoint set via one var makes test-connection pass against a different host than real sends.
gaps.fixSummary: Standardise on SABSMS_GUPSHUP_BASE_URL in internal.rs::test_gupshup (accept the old name as fallback), and note the var in .env.example.

gaps.title: Webhook URL-secret comparison is non-constant-time
gaps.detail: webhook.rs:192 — `doc_secret != given` plain string compare on the per-account webhookSecret, which is the ONLY auth factor for MSG91/Gupshup webhooks. Theoretical timing side-channel on a public unauthenticated route.
gaps.fixSummary: Use a constant-time comparison (e.g. subtle::ConstantTimeEq over the bytes, or compare SHA-256 digests) in handle_account.

gaps.title: Telnyx synchronous cost truncated to integer cents — sub-cent wholesale prices recorded as 0
gaps.detail: telnyx.rs:186-191 maps cost.amount ("0.004") to cents via (d*100).round() as i64 → 0; its own test pins this: `assert_eq!(r.cost, Some(0)); // 0.004 USD rounds to 0 cents` (telnyx.rs:511). US SMS wholesale is sub-cent, so every Telnyx send records 0 cost, poisoning the V2.10 cost-vs-price margin analytics that this field exists to feed.
gaps.fixSummary: Store cost in micro-dollars or thousandths of a cent (i64) on SendResult + message doc (rename to costMicros), and convert at display time; update the telnyx test pin and any worker writes of the cost field.

verifiedOk: Telnyx adapter fully real: POST /v2/messages send with messaging_profile_id/media/callback, 429→Throttled, 401/403→InvalidCredentials, error-code extraction, Ed25519 webhook verification over '{ts}|{body}' vs blob.publicKey, inbound/DLR parsers — with wiremock + signed-fixture tests (services/sabsms-engine/src/providers/telnyx.rs:87-553)

MSG91 adapter fully real: v5 JSON send with authkey header, DLT PE_ID/DLT_TE_ID precedence, textual-error passthrough for substring mapping, form/JSON/nested-report DLR parsing, inbound parsing; wiremock tests (msg91.rs:131-436)

Gupshup adapter fully real: GatewayAPI/rest form send with mask + principalEntityId/dltTemplateId, 101/102→InvalidCredentials, GET-style DLR/inbound parsing with cause keywords; wiremock tests incl. RBM and capability paths (gupshup.rs:273-745)
registry.rs static adapter registry resolves twilio/telnyx/msg91/gupshup/mock and returns None for vonage/plivo/aws_sns, used by both worker and webhook dispatch (registry.rs:28-48 + tests)

errors_map.rs normalization done properly: closed code set, per-provider exact tables (Twilio 21211/21610/30003-30008/20429, Telnyx 4000x, MSG91 9/16/17/28, Gupshup cause keywords + 105/106/175), substring fallback for textual errors, retryable+suppress flags, table-driven tests (errors_map.rs:38-270)

Suppress semantics enforced on BOTH paths: worker sync-rejection → suppress_destination upsert into sabsms_suppressions (worker.rs:924-956, 218-247) and DLR → suppress_from_dlr with reason=normalizedCode, source=carrier_block (handlers/webhook.rs:589-647, 689-718); normalizedCode stored on the message doc alongside raw errorCode (webhook.rs:594)

Generic webhook route GET|POST /webhook/:provider/:account_id/:direction registered (handlers/mod.rs:70-78, with the axum :param-syntax trap documented); account-doc pinning of workspace + provider-mismatch rejection (webhook.rs:139-147); auth = provider signature OR per-account ?secret= webhookSecret (webhook.rs:174-196); Gupshup GET callbacks handled by treating the query string as payload (webhook.rs:162-172); legacy Twilio route kept (mod.rs:72)

gaps.title: Routing preview endpoint hardcodes the fallback candidate to Twilio
gaps.detail: internal.rs:107: `doc_provider: Some(ProviderId::Twilio)` in preview_route. Today this mirrors the real enqueue default (handlers/send.rs:68 `input.provider.unwrap_or(ProviderId::Twilio)`, and no Next-side enqueueSend caller passes provider/providerAccountId — verified across send/quick-send/campaigns/inbox actions). But for a workspace whose only accounts are e.g. MSG91/Telnyx, the trace renders a 'twilio (workspace default)' fallback candidate (routing/page.tsx:431) that on a real send would fail creds resolution with no_credentials and be advanced past — the preview overstates the safety net. Known suspect confirmed, severity minor since it matches current enqueue behavior.
gaps.fixSummary: In preview_route, look up the workspace's default provider account (sabsms_provider_accounts, isDefault: true) and use its provider for the fallback RoutingContext, falling back to Twilio only when none exists; optionally annotate the fallback candidate with credsResolvable so the UI can grey it out.

gaps.title: Circuit gate never applies to the default-account fallback candidate, so the breaker cannot stop default-route traffic
gaps.detail: worker.rs:641 gates only `if let Some(acct) = cand.provider_account_id` — the fallback candidate (provider_account_id None, mod.rs:177-186) bypasses the gate entirely, yet failures ARE recorded against the resolved default account id (worker.rs:926-931 via resolved_creds.account_id). Net effect: for the dominant case (no routing rule, doc has no explicit account — true of every current Next enqueue caller), a circuit that opens for the default account never actually skips a send; the breaker only protects rule-routed traffic. Defensible as 'never lose messages' (the candidate list would otherwise be empty), but it means the V2.6 circuit gives zero protection to unrouted workspaces while the health page shows their circuit as 'open'.
gaps.fixSummary: In the candidate loop, resolve creds before the circuit gate and gate on resolved_creds.account_id instead of cand.provider_account_id, with an escape hatch: if this is the LAST viable candidate, downgrade Skip to Probe/Allow (or schedule a delayed retry instead of attempting) so messages are still never dropped.

gaps.title: Conversion stats invisible for env-default-creds workspaces; attribution can credit the wrong account
gaps.detail: predict_account (otp/mod.rs:288-320) resolves to the literal account id 'default' when no workspace provider account resolves (env-creds path, creds.rs:227+ returns account_id: None). otpstats keys then use acct='default', but GET /v1/otp/stats (otp/mod.rs:516-528) only scans keys for account ids found in COL_PROVIDER_ACCOUNTS for the workspace — so such workspaces send/verify OTPs yet see a permanently empty conversion tab, and 'default'-keyed conversions never feed any account's otp_score for routing. Also predict_account hardcodes doc_provider=Twilio (otp/mod.rs:294-298), so when the worker's actual routing diverges (failover, non-Twilio default), sent/converted accrue to the predicted account, not the carrier that actually sent — acknowledged as 'approximate' in the comment (otp/mod.rs:285-287) but it skews the very signal the router ranks on.
gaps.fixSummary: In otp::stats, additionally scan acct='default' keys filtered to... (cannot tenant-scope; instead) write otpstats under a workspace-scoped pseudo-account id (e.g. `env:{workspaceId}`) in predict_account when creds resolve to env defaults, and include that id in the stats account list. For attribution drift, have the worker bump a corrective stat (or store actual account id back onto the OTP Redis record after send) so verify credits the real sender.

gaps.title: Full-keyspace Redis SCANs in /v1/otp/stats (per account, per page load) and the 60s fraud ticker
gaps.detail: otp::stats runs SCAN MATCH sabsms:otpstats:{acct}:* once per workspace provider account (otp/mod.rs:533-568) — SCAN iterates the entire keyspace server-side regardless of MATCH, so a workspace with 5 accounts = 5 full keyspace walks per dashboard load. fraud::tick does the same once per 60s (fraud.rs:317-340). Fine at current scale; degrades linearly with total Redis keys (queues, dedupe, links, journeys all share the instance).
gaps.fixSummary: Maintain a SET index of live otpstats keys (SADD on incr_otp_field in routing/health.rs, expire alongside) and SMEMBERS it in both readers instead of SCAN.

gaps.title: seal_code silently degrades to base64 plaintext when SABSMS_CREDS_KEY is unset/malformed
gaps.detail: store.rs:221-239: with no valid 64-hex SABSMS_CREDS_KEY, the resend copy is stored as 'plain.'+base64(code) with only a tracing::warn — in a misconfigured prod this quietly undermines the 'hashed codes in Redis' guarantee (the salted hash is intact, but a trivially-decodable copy sits beside it). .env.example:42 lists the var but nothing enforces it at boot.
gaps.fixSummary: In config::Config::from_env (or main.rs startup), warn-and-refuse (or at least error-log once at boot) when SABSMS_CREDS_KEY is absent outside dev; alternatively make seal_code return None and have resend fall back to issuing a fresh code instead of storing plaintext.

gaps.title: Template DLT binding is dead at send time — no send path forwards dltTemplateId/dltEntityId from the bound template doc into EnqueueSendInput
gaps.detail: The scrub panel DOES persist the binding onto the template doc (templates/[id]/actions.ts:83-87 writes doc.dlt.{principalEntityId,templateId,headerId,contentCategory}), and the engine pipe is ready end-to-end (EnqueueSendInput.dltEntityId/dltTemplateId in types.ts:496-498; handlers/send.rs:93-94 persists them; worker send_options_from_doc worker.rs:88-99 maps them to SendOptions.dlt). But NOTHING reads the binding at send time: sendSmsAction loads the template doc (send/actions.ts:106-115) yet its enqueueSend call (send/actions.ts:199-210) omits dlt fields; the public API route does the same (api/v1/sms/messages/route.ts:118-177); campaign fan-out builds message docs with no dlt fields at all (campaigns.rs:646-674); journeys/inbox/CRM-dispatch/sabflow paths have zero dlt references (grep across all enqueueSend call sites returned nothing). The only mechanism that ever attaches DLT params is the worker's auto-attach heuristic (worker.rs:177-213), which re-derives by scrubbing the body against ALL registry templates and attaches only on an exactly-one match — it NEVER reads the template doc binding. dlt-scrub-panel.tsx:12-15 explicitly claims the binding persists 'which the engine's auto-attach reads at send time' — that claim is false. Consequence: with 2+ registry templates whose bodies both match (similar OTP/txn templates are common), or a body that drifts from the registered text, the explicit binding is silently dropped → IN marketing sends block with dlt_template_required and transactional sends go to MSG91/Gupshup without PE_ID/DLT_TE_ID, risking operator rejection. The differentiator's headline flow (bind in editor → send → engine receives dlt params) does not work as specified.

gaps.title: Journey send-step enqueue failures kill the run permanently — no transient-error retry
gaps.detail: executor.ts:192-201: any `executeSendStep` failure sets the run to status 'failed' + completedAt — terminal. executor.ts:358-363 funnels ALL enqueue exceptions (engine restart, network blip, 5xx from engineFetch) into that same path, with no transient/permanent classification, no backoff, no retry count. A 10-second engine restart during a tick permanently fails every journey run that hit a send step in that window. Worse, the engine does NOT gracefully dedupe replays as executor.ts:13-14 claims: handlers/send.rs:113-116 is a plain insert_one, so a duplicate idempotencyKey (e.g. two workers after a >5-min stale reclaim, store.ts:44) raises E11000 → error → the second worker terminally fails a run the first worker is still executing correctly. Only template_missing/missing_vars (executor.ts:317-325) deserve terminal failure.
gaps.fixSummary: In executor.ts executeSendStep, classify failures: template_missing/missing_vars stay terminal; enqueue exceptions re-park the run (status 'waiting', wakeAt now+backoff, retryCount in run doc capped at ~5 before failing) instead of status 'failed'. Treat engine E11000/409 on the idempotency key as success-skip (message already exists). Optionally make engine send.rs catch the duplicate-key error and return the existing message id. Touch executor.ts, types.ts (retry field), memory-store + executor.test.ts.

gaps.title: Pinpoint importer drops NextActivity sequencing — branch chains fall through into sibling branches at runtime
gaps.detail: pinpoint.ts:237-253 maps SMS activities to `{kind:'send'}` steps WITHOUT capturing `SMS.NextActivity`; same for Wait (:255-282). Step order is BFS from StartActivity (orderActivities, :148-183), and the executor advances send/wait steps by ARRAY ORDER (executor.ts:206 nextStepIdAfter). For any journey where TrueActivity's chain has 2+ activities, BFS interleaves branches (split→[A1,B1,A2]), so after A1's send the run falls through into B1 — the FALSE branch — sending the wrong sequence. Both fixtures (pinpoint-welcome.json, pinpoint-winback.json) happen to avoid this shape, so pinpoint.test.ts (168 lines) never exercises it; imports of real multi-step branched journeys will execute incorrectly while looking valid. validate.ts emits no warning for it either.
gaps.fixSummary: Capture NextActivity in the importer and linearize correctly: either (a) topologically order each branch chain and insert explicit `branch`-style jump steps (a 1-condition always-true branch) where fallthrough would cross chains, or (b) add an optional `nextStepId` to JourneySendStep/JourneyWaitStep honored by nextStepIdAfter in executor.ts + validate.ts reachability. Add a fixture with a 2-step true branch followed by a false branch and assert execution order in pinpoint.test.ts.

gaps.title: Executor ignores EnqueueSendResult.status — suppressed/engine-disabled sends recorded as 'sent', run marches on
gaps.detail: executor.ts:347-380 never inspects `res.status`. The engine returns `{id:'', status:'suppressed'}` for suppressed recipients (send.rs:59-67) and the client short-circuits the same stub when the engine is disabled (engine-client.ts:270-273). The executor then pushes history 'sent' with messageId undefined, increments stats.sends (executor.ts:373-379), and continues the journey — inflating send counts and walking a suppressed contact through waitUntil/branch steps. No message leaks (the engine blocks each send), but stats and run history misrepresent reality; the run should exit.
gaps.fixSummary: In executeSendStep, check res.status: on 'suppressed', exit the run (status 'exited', history 'exit:suppressed', incJourneyStats exited) instead of recording 'sent'; only inc sends when an id is returned. Add a memory-store test.

gaps.title: No nightly reconciliation or identity job is actually scheduled — cost page shows $0 and 30d counters/histograms never decay without manual runs
gaps.detail: Plan requires 'backfill script + nightly reconciliation job' and 'nightly histogram/affinity cron'. Both scripts exist and are real, but nothing schedules them: ecosystem.config.js registers only sabsms-engine (line 142) and sabsms-events (line 179); scripts/cron-worker.mjs and src/app/api/cron/* have zero sabsms entries. sabsms-identity-nightly.mjs only documents a manual PM2 command in its docstring (lines 29-34); sabsms-backfill-stats.mjs documents no scheduling at all. Consequence chain: rollups.ts:23-26 — the live consumer leaves costCents/creditsSpent at 0 ('the backfill script and reconcile fill them'), so /sabsms/analytics/cost KPIs/day-series/provider rows show $0 cost and 0 credits for every day nobody manually reconciled; graph.ts '30d' counters are actually 'since first touch' and histograms never decay (identity-nightly docstring lines 7-22 admits this is the backstop).
gaps.fixSummary: Add two PM2 cron apps to ecosystem.config.js (pattern from the docstrings: tsx + NODE_PATH stub, --cron-restart '30 2 * * *' / '45 2 * * *', --no-autorestart) for sabsms-identity-nightly.mjs and sabsms-backfill-stats.mjs --days 3; or add /api/cron/sabsms-stats-reconcile + /api/cron/sabsms-identity-nightly jobs to the existing cron-worker registry.

Selecting a provider chip changes the long-tail tiles but leaves all 8 KPI cards and the funnel unfiltered — the dashboard silently shows inconsistent numbers.
gaps.fixSummary: For provider/campaign: query the single-dim rollup rows and filter buckets to the selection (then derive KPIs/series/funnel from those rows instead of the total rows). For country: no rollup dim exists — either fall back to the raw aggregation path when a country filter is active, or visibly disable headline filtering for country. Files: src/app/sabsms/analytics/page.tsx (loadAll), optionally rollups.ts to accept a dims-value filter.

gaps.title: Deliverability subpage mixes real aggregations with hardcoded proxy constants presented as live metrics
gaps.detail: deliverability/page.tsx: regionalPerformanceData uses `dlr: 95, // Proxy` and `latency: 1.5` for EVERY region (lines 55-59); tableDataTemplateDLR uses `dlr: 99, // Proxy` and a fake alternating `trend: i % 2 === 0 ? 'up' : 'down'` (lines 61-67); failureCodeData collapses all failures into one 'Failed' bucket (51-53) despite runErrorPareto existing one directory up; dlrTrendData (69-75) maps the SAME window-aggregate provider dlrRate onto every time point, rendering flat fake 'trend' lines (and defaults missing providers to 95).

Plan-faithful option is to wire it. Files: src/app/sabsms/analytics/funnel/page.tsx (new), client.tsx (accept props), cohorts/page.tsx (keep link).

gaps.title: No bot/HEAD click filtering anywhere — bots inflate click rollups/CTR and poison identity send-time histograms (smart-send input)
gaps.detail: Known suspect CONFIRMED: s/[shortCode]/page.tsx:50-51 'bots redirect AND count (UA is stored — filtering happens in analytics later)' — that 'later' never happened. recordClick (links.ts:~195-215) stores userAgent but never inspects it; rollups bumpStats counts every linkClicked (rollups.ts:162-163); reconcile counts every sabsms_link_clicks doc (reconcile.ts:156-161); touchIdentity bumps clicks30d AND sendTimeHistogram[hour] for every click (graph.ts:204-227). Carrier link-preview/security scanners (which fetch within seconds of send) therefore inflate CTR and teach the histogram that the SEND hour is the 'best hour' — directly corrupting the smart-send differentiator.
gaps.fixSummary: Add an isLikelyBot(ua) helper (bot|crawl|spider|preview|facebookexternalhit|WhatsApp|Slackbot|HeadlessChrome + empty UA) in links-core.ts; in recordClick persist `bot: true` instead of skipping (keeps redirect analytics), suppress emitLinkClickedEvent for bot clicks, and exclude bot:true in reconcile's clicks query + the click countDocuments in aggregations.ts. Files: src/lib/sabsms/links-core.ts, links.ts, analytics/reconcile.ts, analytics/aggregations.ts.

gaps.title: runProviderScorecard $pushes every message latency into one accumulator array — BSON-limit/memory blow-up risk; <500ms accept criterion not met for long-tail tiles on big workspaces
gaps.detail: aggregations.ts:412-425: the provider scorecard `$push`es a latency entry for EVERY outbound message in the window into a per-provider array, then sorts in JS (436-441). On the plan's own accept target (1M-message workspace, 90d window) this is a multi-hundred-MB group accumulator that will exceed the 16MB BSON document limit or allowDiskUse-crawl; it runs on EVERY analytics page load (page.tsx:268, also deliverability page). Seven other raw aggregations (cohort, countries, topContacts, costVsRevenue, templateReplies, errorPareto) also still scan sabsms_messages per load — admitted in the page help text, but the p95 $push one is the correctness/availability risk.
gaps.fixSummary: Replace $push+JS-sort with Mongo `$percentile` (p95, available 7.0+) or a $bucket histogram accumulator; alternatively precompute provider latency into the rollups via a new counter pair (latencySumMs/deliveredWithLatency) on messageDelivered. Files: src/app/sabsms/analytics/aggregations.ts (runProviderScorecard).

gaps.title: RCS flat-rate pricing is dead at the credits route — RCS sends are billed as per-segment SMS
gaps.detail: The engine worker sends channel:"rcs" in the credit reserve body (worker.rs:546-552), and the Rust DTO comment even concedes the truth: "Additive — the Next credits route tolerates the extra field" (types.rs:203-207). The Next route IGNORES it: route.ts:79-85 hardcodes channel:'sms' under the now-stale comment "The reserve body has no channel — 'sms' for now (derive later)" (same at route.ts:131 for reserve-batch). The TS type carries the field (types.ts:521-522 "RCS is flat 1") and rates.ts:54-55 defines rcsFlatPerMessage:1, but that branch is unreachable on the live reserve path. Result: an RCS message with a 3-segment fallback body to a default-rate country reserves and charges 6 credits instead of flat 1 (rates.ts:34 defaultPerSegment:2); 2-segment India RCS charges 2 instead of 1. Billing wrong, and the half-wire is documented in-code as if acceptable.
gaps.fixSummary: In route.ts handleOp('reserve'), read body.channel, validate to 'sms'|'mms'|'rcs', and pass it to creditCostForWorkspace instead of the literal 'sms'; delete the stale comment. Secondary: the in-loop RCS→SMS provider-rejection fallback (worker.rs:898-917) keeps the already-taken flat-RCS reservation while delivering per-segment SMS — either re-reserve on fallback or document the accepted undercharge in worker.rs.

gaps.title: ContentSid cache is in-process per worker (plan implied shared/Redis)
gaps.detail: twilio.rs:23-30: static Lazy<Mutex<HashMap>> keyed {accountSid}:{payload hash} with 24h TTL. Multi-worker deployments will create duplicate Content resources (Twilio-side clutter + extra API calls) but no correctness issue. Acceptable deferral.
gaps.fixSummary: Optional: thread a redis handle (or a small KV trait) into the adapter constructor via registry.rs and back the cache with Redis SETEX under the same key/TTL; otherwise keep with the documented rationale.
