This fake-AI button renders in the SAME composer pane as the real V2.12 agent suggestion card (thread-view.tsx:563-598, fed by conversation.aiSuggestion written by runtime.ts suggest mode). Two Sparkles-branded AI affordances, one fake — exactly the confusing dual-render the suspect list predicted, and a feature presented as working AI that is static text.
gaps.fixSummary: Delete generateAiReply + the 'AI Suggest' button, or reimplement it as a real on-demand suggestion: a server action that loads the thread, builds the same scrubbed transcript/knowledge context, and calls the gateway (generateSabcrmText, or runAgentTurn in suggest mode against createMongoAgentStore) then writes aiSuggestion so the existing card renders it. Touch: src/app/sabsms/inbox/actions.ts, thread-view.tsx.

gaps.title: Opt-out guardrail only covers marketing-last-outbound threads — agent's own 'service' replies remove the thread from guardrail scope
gaps.detail: handlers.ts:52 MARKETING_CATEGORIES={'marketing'} and handlers.ts:124-132 skip classification unless the LAST outbound message is category 'marketing'. But every agent auto-reply is enqueued as category:'service' (runtime.ts:392). So on a marketing thread: turn 1 the customer asks a question, agent replies (service); turn 2 the customer says 'please don't text me anymore' — runOptOutGuardrail returns {intent:'skipped'} (lastOutbound.category==='service'), no suppression, isSuppressed misses (runtime.ts:209), and the auto agent replies normally. This violates the phase's headline acceptance ('please don't text me anymore' → suppressed, no agent reply) in the agent's primary regime — multi-turn agent conversations. Untested: all 15 opt_out eval fixtures seed conversationCategory:'marketing' with a single seed outbound (fixtures Counter: ('opt_out','marketing'):15), never an agent-service-reply-then-opt-out sequence.
gaps.fixSummary: Widen 'marketing-ish' in runOptOutGuardrail: classify when ANY recent outbound (e.g. last 10 via a new store.listRecentOutbound or reuse listThreadMessages) is marketing, OR when the conversation has agent turns / aiFlags (agent-driven thread), or simply always-classify when the workspace agent is enabled. Add eval fixtures: marketing seed → agent service reply → natural-language opt-out must suppress. Touch: src/lib/sabsms/agent/handlers.ts, store.ts (if new accessor), scripts/fixtures/sabsms-agent-eval.json.

gaps.title: sabsms-insights-nightly not registered in ecosystem.config.js — insights never mined in production, inbox card permanently empty
gaps.detail: ecosystem.config.js contains only sabsms-engine (line 142) and sabsms-events (line 179) — no sabsms-insights-nightly (and no sabsms-identity-nightly either). The only 'registration' is a comment in the script header (sabsms-insights-nightly.mjs:22-27) telling an operator to hand-run `pm2 start ... --cron-restart "15 3 * * *"`. Repo-wide grep for insights-nightly finds no runbook, package.json script, or deploy hook. Result: sabsms_conversation_insights is never written on a fresh deploy, loadInboxInsights returns null forever, and the InboxInsightsStrip (page.tsx:85) silently renders nothing while the feature is presented as shipped.
gaps.fixSummary: Add a PM2 app entry to ecosystem.config.js: script './node_modules/.bin/tsx', args 'scripts/sabsms-insights-nightly.mjs', cron_restart '15 3 * * *', autorestart:false, env { MONGODB_URI, MONGODB_DB, NODE_PATH:'./src/workers/_stubs', LLM keys }. Add the analogous entry for sabsms-identity-nightly (V2.10 has the same hole) while there.

gaps.title: Worker-safe credit mirror duplicates ledger.ts atomic movement (billing drift risk)
gaps.detail: chargeAgentTurnCredit (store.ts:480-539) reimplements the canonical ledger movement from credits/ledger.ts:164-207 — same conditional `{'credits.sms':{$gte}}` + `$inc` on users, same sabsms_credit_reservations + sabsms_credit_ledger collections — because ledger.ts is server-only + @/-coupled and can't load in the tsx worker (acknowledged at store.ts:18-24). Two independent copies of billing mechanics WILL drift: balanceAfter recipe already differs (ledger.ts appendLedger uses getSmsCreditBalance with 0-fallback; store.ts reads raw with undefined-fallback), reservation lifecycle fields differ (no expiresAt TTL semantics on the instant-finalise row), and any future change (rate-card costing in V2.13, kind enum, schema fields) must be hand-mirrored or audits silently desync.
gaps.fixSummary: Extract a worker-safe core: src/lib/sabsms/credits/core.ts (no 'server-only', no '@/' — pure functions taking a Db: reserveMovement, finaliseMovement, instantDebit). ledger.ts becomes a thin server-only wrapper (connectToDatabase + core); agent/store.ts chargeAgentTurnCredit delegates to core.instantDebit. Single source of truth for the atomic $inc + reservation + ledger-row recipe.

gaps.title: Rollout controls incomplete: no plan gate on the agent, autoSegments dead config, no per-conversation auto opt-in
gaps.detail: Plan: 'suggested replies → opt-in full-auto per conversation/segment, behind plan gate.' (a) No plan/admin gate exists: saveAgentConfigAction (agent-actions.ts:66-77) is only RBAC sabsms_settings — any workspace on any plan can flip auto mode on. V2.11 RCS set the documented precedent of a workspace-boolean gate when plan flags were missing (types.ts:465-470, send/actions.ts:296-300); the agent has not even that. (b) autoSegments is declared (store.ts:53) and projected from Mongo (store.ts:312-314) but NEVER read by runtime.ts (grep confirms zero consumers) and has no settings UI — full-auto is workspace-global; segment-scoped rollout is dead config. (c) No per-conversation auto opt-in/opt-out either (only the post-hoc handoff flag).
gaps.fixSummary: Enforce autoSegments in runAgentTurn: when config.mode==='auto' and autoSegments is non-empty, check contact segment membership (new store accessor) and degrade to suggest when outside. Add an agentEnabled workspace/plan gate boolean mirroring the rcsEnabled pattern, checked in getConfig or the handlers. Expose autoSegments + gate state in agent-card.tsx / agent-actions.ts.

gaps.title: Agent turn debit has no distinct charge type in the ledger
gaps.detail: Plan: 'agent turns metered as a credit charge type'. Implementation writes kind:'debit' with synthetic messageId 'agent:{turnId}' (store.ts:529-537) — distinguishable from SMS message debits only by string-prefix convention; any billing report grouping by `kind` lumps agent turns with sends.
gaps.fixSummary: Add an explicit field (e.g. chargeType:'agent_turn') to the ledger row in chargeAgentTurnCredit and to the SabsmsCreditLedgerRow type in ledger.ts (optional, default 'message'), so reports can segment agent spend without string parsing.

gaps.title: Auto turn charged before LLM is never refunded on error; engine-disabled stub still audits 'replied'
gaps.detail: Credit is debited before the LLM call (runtime.ts:238-242, by design fail-closed) and the reservation is instantly finalised (store.ts:508-517) — so an LLM failure (runtime.ts:283-285) or enqueue failure (runtime.ts:415-418) keeps the customer's credit with outcome 'error' and no release path. Also, with SABSMS_ENABLED=false enqueueSend returns {id:'',status:'suppressed'} (engine-client.ts:270-273) and the turn still audits 'replied' (replyMessageId undefined) with the credit kept.
gaps.fixSummary: On the error paths after a successful charge, refund: add store.releaseAgentTurnCredit(workspaceId, turnId) (reverse $inc + 'release' ledger row keyed on the synthetic messageId — trivial once the shared credits core from the dedupe fix exists) and call it in runAgentTurn's LLM-error/enqueue-error branches; treat status==='suppressed' with empty id as a guarded/error outcome rather than 'replied'.

verifiedOk: Regex opt-out pre-pass: guardrails.ts:97-136 pure both-direction short-circuit (opt-out / not-opt-out / unclear / null→LLM), unit-tested in agent/__tests__/guardrails.test.ts

scrubPii: guardrails.ts:52-80 reversible masking of phones/emails/12+ digit runs with restore(); applied at every LLM exit point — classifier (guardrails.ts:174), agent transcript + inbound (runtime.ts:116,260), insights mining (mining.ts:255), composer assist (ai-assist.ts:63)

LLM opt-out classifier runs BEFORE the agent: handlers.ts registers guardrail then agent on messageInbound (226,246); SabsmsEventRouter.dispatch awaits handlers sequentially in registration order (consumer.ts:197-203); second defence aiFlags.guardedInboundId checked independently at runtime.ts:203

Guardrail opt-out actions are real: suppression upsert + consent-log row (ai_classified) + TCPA confirmation through sabsmsEngine.enqueueSend with idempotencyKey optout-confirm:{inboundId} (handlers.ts:136-188); flag written BEFORE the send so a crash can't let the agent reply

Agent runtime suggest/auto: suggest parks aiSuggestion on the conversation (runtime.ts:357-367), auto replies through enqueueSend with category 'service', tags ['ai-agent'], idempotencyKey agent:{inboundId}, reply-from = inbound doc's `to` (runtime.ts:370-419) — the full-stack structural guarantee is genuine

Tools: lookupContact/searchKnowledge/handoffToHuman/createNote (tools.ts:109-153) on a bounded structured-JSON tool loop, MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS=3, disallowed tools rejected, results truncated into the audit (runtime.ts:274-339)

Max-turns + handoff: countTurns counts only replied/suggested vs config.maxTurnsPerConversation (runtime.ts:213-219, store.ts:440-447); keyword handoff without LLM call (runtime.ts:222-235); handed-off threads guarded thereafter (runtime.ts:206)

Credit metering fail-closed: auto turns charged BEFORE the LLM call (runtime.ts:237-243); atomic conditional $inc {'credits.sms':{$gte:1}} with replay dedupe on synthetic messageId agent:{turnId} (store.ts:480-539); insufficient credits → guarded, no LLM (tested)

sabsms_agent_turns audit: every non-skipped path inserts a turn row (turnId, tokens, toolCalls, mode, outcome, reason) — replied/suggested/handoff/guarded/error all audited; indexes ensured (store.ts:227-243, handlers.ts:231)
Replay tolerance: hasTurnForInbound skips redelivered inbounds (runtime.ts:158), guardrail re-delivery short-circuit (handlers.ts:120-122), enqueue idempotency keys on both reply and confirmation

Insights mining is real: mining.ts LLM map (batches of 20, 400-conversation cost ceiling) + pure tested reduce (normalizeTopicLabel/mergeTopicBatches/mergeIntentBatches), history-retaining inserts to sabsms_conversation_insights, loadInsightsWithTrend + topicTrend
Nightly miner script: scripts/sabsms-insights-nightly.mjs iterates active workspaces, honest no-op exit 0 without an LLM key, --dry-run/--workspace flags (registration gap flagged separately)

Inbox insights card wired: page.tsx:70-85 loads via loadInboxInsights (actions.ts:782+) and renders InboxInsightsStrip with top-5 topics, sentiment tones, trend arrows vs previous run; renders null honestly when no doc exists

Settings agent card: agent-card.tsx full form (enable, suggest/auto, persona, knowledge, max turns, handoff keyword chips) rendered on settings/page.tsx:45; agent-actions.ts RBAC-gated (sabsms_settings view/edit), sanitized writes to sabsms_agent_configs — the exact doc the worker store reads

Composer AI assist with DLT preservation: ai-assist.ts:44-100 real gateway call (generateSabcrmText), reversible PII scrub, hasDltMarkers instruction + post-hoc markersPreserved rejection (line 90 — never silently returns a mangled template); wired in send/composer.tsx:374-383 with proposal accept/reject UI (702-736); markers.ts pure multiset check, unit-tested

Eval harness = 54 cases: scripts/sabsms-agent-eval.mjs runs the REAL guardrail+runtime code over scripts/fixtures/sabsms-agent-eval.json — 50 conversations (15 opt_out, 10 qa, 10 jailbreak, 10 handoff, 5 unclear) + 4 DLT marker checks; strict assertions on suppression rows, consent rows, TCPA body, audit rows, ledger debits, idempotency keys, noLlm counters; exit 1 gates; optional --live relaxed pass

Quiet-hours posture matches the plan's acceptance: agent replies go out category 'service' which the engine exempts (quiet_hours.rs: 'Marketing only — transactional/otp/alert/service are exempt'), expectation logged per turn (guardrails.ts:201-205, runtime.ts:373-377) — 'agent answers in auto-mode within quiet hours' is satisfied with the engine owning enforcement

Worker-safety + wiring: agent/* and insights/* use relative imports only, no server-only; engine-client lazy-imported (runtime.ts:69-74); registerAgentEventHandlers wired into the production router (consumer.ts:272) which the PM2 sabsms-events app runs (ecosystem.config.js:179); engine MessageInbound payload carries workspace_id/message_id/conversation_id/from/body (events.rs:55-65)

Inbox renders the real V2.12 surfaces: aiSuggestion card with Use/Dismiss + acceptSuggestion audit stamp (thread-view.tsx:563-598, actions.ts:737-774), human reply consumes pending suggestion (actions.ts:360-362), Possible-opt-out and AI-handoff badges (thread-view.tsx:872-881)

Unit tests cover the phase surface: guardrails (pre-pass, scrub, classifier degrade-to-unclear, guardrail orchestration incl. replay), runtime (all guards, metering fail-closed, suggest-never-charges, suggest/auto routing), markers, mining reduce — src/lib/sabsms/{agent,insights}/__tests__/

gaps.title: Rate-card hijack: any workspace can attach any other workspace as a 'child' and re-price its sends
gaps.detail: upsertRateCard validates childWorkspaceIds ONLY for ObjectId validity and self-attachment (ratecards/store.ts:85-93) — there is no parent/child hierarchy, no consent, and no check that the 'reseller' has any relationship with the child. findRateCardForChild resolves the active card purely by `{ childWorkspaceIds: childWorkspaceId, effectiveFrom: { $lte: now } }` (store.ts:170-175), and the credits route prices EVERY send via creditCostForWorkspace (credits/route.ts:84-90 reserve, 127-134 reserve-batch). sanitizeRates has no upper bound on creditsPerSegment (store.ts:59-69, only Math.max(0.01,...)). Net effect: any authenticated workspace can create a card listing a victim workspace id with creditsPerSegment=1000000 and the victim's every send is charged at that rate (pickRateCard latest-effectiveFrom wins across ALL resellers, resolve.ts:59-72). The plan's 'child-workspace wallets on existing hierarchy' (plans/sabsms-v2-beyond-world-class.md:147) has no hierarchy backing it. Billing-correctness + security wrong.
gaps.fixSummary: Introduce a verified parent→child link (e.g. sabsms_workspace_links doc created via child-side consent or admin attach; or reuse an existing team/sub-account hierarchy) and enforce it in upsertRateCard (reject unlinked children) AND in findRateCardForChild (filter candidate cards to the verified parent). Clamp creditsPerSegment to a sane max (e.g. 1000). Touch src/lib/sabsms/ratecards/store.ts, src/app/sabsms/settings/reseller-actions.ts, new src/lib/sabsms/ratecards/links.ts; add a hostile-card unit test in __tests__/ratecards.test.ts.

gaps.title: Public-API mediaSabFileIds is dead: documented + validated, but media is never sent (silently degrades to text-only SMS)
gaps.detail: The route accepts mediaSabFileIds (messages/route.ts:45-48), rejects raw URLs loudly per SabFiles policy (route.ts:58-67), and passes `media: [{ sabFileId, mime: 'application/octet-stream', bytes: 0 }]` to enqueueSend (route.ts:171-175) — but never resolves the SabFiles ids to URLs and never sets `mediaUrls` or `channel: 'mms'`. The engine stores `media` on the doc (handlers/send.rs:81) yet the worker builds provider SendOptions ONLY from `mediaUrls` (worker.rs:78-86, comment: 'Resolved public media URLs (R2) for MMS — pre-resolved Next-side'; types.ts:493-494 confirms the contract) and channel defaults to Sms (handlers/send.rs:68). The OpenAPI spec sells it as working: 'SabFiles file ids for MMS media' (openapi.ts:145-150). An integrator's MMS goes out as a bare SMS with no media and no error — presented-as-working but dead. Also no validation the SabFiles ids exist or belong to the workspace.
gaps.fixSummary: In sendOnce (messages/route.ts): load the SabFiles docs workspace-scoped, 404/422 on missing or foreign ids, resolve each to its public R2 URL, populate mediaUrls + real mime/bytes in media[], and set channel:'mms' when media present (mirror whatever the campaigns MMS path does). Alternatively, if MMS is out of scope, remove mediaSabFileIds from SEND_BODY and from the OpenAPI spec instead of documenting a no-op. Add an e2e step asserting the engine doc carries mediaUrls.

gaps.title: sk_test_ sandbox keys deferred — no test mode on the developer platform
gaps.detail: Only SABSMS_KEY_LIVE_PREFIX = 'sk_live_' exists (core.ts:34); authenticateApiKey hard-rejects anything else (store.ts:188), and the unit test explicitly asserts sk_test_ keys are malformed (__tests__/apikeys-core.test.ts:49). There is no way for an integrator to develop against the API without spending real credits and sending real SMS. Sandbox mode is table stakes for a dev platform, and the engine already has the hook: SABSMS_PROVIDER_MOCK test provider exists (services/sabsms-engine/src/worker.rs:64-68, types.rs:64).
gaps.fixSummary: Add sk_test_ minting (mode flag on mintApiKey + key doc `mode: 'live'|'test'`), accept both prefixes in authenticateApiKey, thread auth.mode through withSabsmsApi, and on test-mode sends pass a per-message mock/test flag to enqueueSend so the engine routes to the mock provider (extend EnqueueSendInput + worker provider pick beyond the global SABSMS_PROVIDER_MOCK env) and the credits reserve is skipped or zero-rated; tag resulting messages/analytics as test. Touch core.ts, store.ts, http.ts, messages+verify routes, engine types.rs/worker.rs, api-keys page (mode picker).

gaps.title: OTP drop-in widget (hosted JS + iframe on public verify API) missing — public-key acceptance criterion unmeetable
gaps.detail: Plan V2.13 (plans/sabsms-v2-beyond-world-class.md:146) requires an 'OTP drop-in widget (hosted JS + iframe on public verify API)' and the acceptance (line 148) is 'external script sends + verifies OTP with only a public key'. No widget asset exists (grep for drop-in/widget in sabsms + public/ finds only the unrelated SabChat embed/widget.js and sabsense-snippet.js); there is no publishable pk_ key concept anywhere (scopes.ts has only 5 secret-key scopes; core.ts mints sk_live_ only), and the verify routes require Bearer sk_live_ secrets (verify/send/route.ts:18) — which can never be embedded in a browser. V2.7's deferral note (plan line 108) pointed here, so V2.13 was the bill-due phase.
gaps.fixSummary: Add publishable keys (pk_live_/pk_test_ prefix, domain/origin allowlist instead of IP allowlist, otp-widget-only scope) in apikeys core/store; new browser-facing endpoints (or origin-checked variants of verify/send+check with strict per-destination+per-origin rate limits); hosted widget script public/sabsms-otp-widget.js + iframe page src/app/sabsms/widget/otp/page.tsx posting to the public API; document on api-docs.

gaps.title: Zero E2E coverage for V2.13 — neither acceptance criterion is exercised anywhere
gaps.detail: The plan's verification strategy says the e2e script is 'grown per phase' (plan line 162), and V2.13's acceptance requires an external-script OTP flow and a margin-report-matches-ledger check (plan line 148). scripts/sabsms-e2e.mjs contains no public-API steps at all: grep for v1/sms, sk_live, apikey, openapi, ratecard returns nothing (only the V2.0-era inbound provider webhook + suppression steps exist, e2e lines 143/268-294). The V2.13 unit tests (apikeys-core, openapi-spec, ratecards, webhooks-out-core, idempotency-replay) are real but pure — nothing proves the key-auth → rate-limit → engine → credits → webhook chain end to end, and the rate-card 2x-cost/margin reconciliation is entirely unverified.
gaps.fixSummary: Extend scripts/sabsms-e2e.mjs with a V2.13 section: mint a key via the store, hit POST/GET /api/v1/sms/messages with Bearer + Idempotency-Key replay assert, verify/send+check against the mock provider, suppression add/delete, analytics summary, webhook endpoint + local HTTPS receiver asserting X-Sabsms-Signature, and a rate-card step (create card at 2x, send as child, assert ledger debit == card price and marginReport row matches).

gaps.title: Reseller engine missing white-label toggles and carrier-fee pass-through line items
gaps.detail: Plan line 147 specifies 'per-tenant price per country/channel/category + carrier-fee pass-through line items ... white-label toggles'. Rate rows carry only `creditsPerSegment` (resolve.ts:33-41) — there is no carrier-fee field and no pass-through line item written to the ledger at finalise. White-label does not exist anywhere in sabsms (case-insensitive grep for white-label across src/app/sabsms + src/lib/sabsms returns nothing). Per-country/channel/category pricing itself IS implemented; these two named sub-features are absent.
gaps.fixSummary: Add optional `carrierFeeCreditsPerMessage` (or cents) to SabsmsRateCardRate + sanitizeRates + creditCostWithCard, and emit a separate ledger line item kind ('carrier_fee') in finaliseCredits so the margin report can itemise it. Add a whiteLabel block (brand name/logo/hide-SabNode toggles) to a reseller settings doc + read it in child-workspace SabSMS shell. Touch resolve.ts, store.ts, reseller-card.tsx, credits/ledger.ts.

gaps.title: Margin report displays credits vs USD cents but never computes the margin (unit mismatch)
gaps.detail: MarginReportRow has creditsCharged (credits) and costCents (USD cents) with no conversion (store.ts:202-210); the UI renders the two columns side by side — 'Credits charged' and 'Wholesale cost' as currency (reseller-card.tsx:402-419) — but there is no margin (price − cost) column because the units are incommensurable. The plan's 'margin report (price − cost)' is therefore only half-delivered: the data is real, the subtraction is impossible. The 'matches ledger to the cent' acceptance can't be evaluated.
gaps.fixSummary: Add a creditValueCents setting (per rate card or per reseller workspace) and compute marginCents = creditsCharged*creditValueCents − costCents in marginReport + a Margin column in reseller-card.tsx.

gaps.title: Suppression writes ride messages:send — no dedicated suppressions scope
gaps.detail: POST /suppressions and DELETE /suppressions/:phone both require 'messages:send' (suppressions/route.ts:49, [phone]/route.ts:14-15); the scope catalogue (scopes.ts:9-15) has no 'suppressions' scope. A key minted only to send messages can also unsuppress opted-out numbers — coarser than ideal for a compliance-sensitive write (DELETE re-enables sending to someone who opted out). Documented consistently in the OpenAPI spec (openapi.ts:407/437/467), so it is honest, just coarse.
gaps.fixSummary: Add 'suppressions:write' to SABSMS_API_SCOPES, switch the two write routes + spec x-scopes to it, keep messages:send accepted for back-compat during a deprecation window (hasScope check either).

gaps.title: Outbound webhook URL validation allows private/internal targets (limited SSRF surface)
gaps.detail: validateWebhookUrl blocks only non-https, 'localhost' and '127.0.0.1' (events.ts:52-66). `https://10.0.0.5/`, `https://[::1]:8443/`, `https://169.254.169.254/`, internal DNS names, and 127.0.0.1.nip.io all pass, and the worker POSTs to them and stores a 256-char responseSnippet visible to the workspace owner (dispatch.ts:292-293) — an internal-network HTTPS probe primitive. Mitigated by the https-only rule (most internal services here — engine, PostgREST, Redis — are plain http), hence minor rather than critical.
gaps.fixSummary: In validateWebhookUrl reject literal private/reserved IPs (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, 169.254/16, ::1, fc00::/7) and add a dispatch-time DNS-resolution check in attemptDelivery (resolve hook.url host, refuse private ranges) to defeat rebinding; unit-test in webhook-urls.test.ts.

gaps.title: Per-key rate limit is in-memory single-process; IP allowlist trusts spoofable XFF
gaps.detail: withSabsmsApi reuses the SabFlow limiter (http.ts:89-92) which is explicitly 'Single-process only — entries live in a module-level Map and are lost on restart' (sabflow/apiRateLimit/store.ts:1-14). Under PM2 cluster / multiple Next instances the effective limit is N x rateLimitPerMin and resets on every deploy. The plan prescribed this exact reuse (plan line 145), so it is an accepted tradeoff, but the api-keys UI presents the limit as authoritative. Relatedly, clientIpOf trusts the first x-forwarded-for hop (apikeys/store.ts:166-173) — if the Next port is ever reachable without the proxy, the IP allowlist is spoofable.
gaps.fixSummary: Swap checkRateLimit's backing store for Redis (sliding window via ZADD/EXPIRE or token bucket, keyed sabsms:key:<id>) behind the same function signature, and document/enforce that nginx overwrites X-Forwarded-For (or read a trusted real-ip header only).

gaps.title: Test-fire/replay run an unscoped global delivery tick — misleading results and cross-tenant work in-request
gaps.detail: testFireEndpoint (actions.ts:302) and replayDelivery (actions.ts:324) call tickWebhookDeliveries({ db }) which claims up to 10 oldest due deliveries from ANY workspace (dispatch.ts:225-231) inside the user's server action. Consequences: (a) the success check `tick.delivered > 0` (actions.ts:304) can be satisfied by an unrelated delivery while the ping actually failed; (b) if 10+ older deliveries are due, the ping isn't attempted at all and the user gets the 'did not return 2xx' error even though it was never tried; (c) other tenants' endpoint POSTs execute on the web process in request context.
gaps.fixSummary: Add an optional `onlyDeliveryId` (or filter) param to tickWebhookDeliveries that restricts the claim query to `{ _id }`, and have testFireEndpoint/replayDelivery pass the specific delivery id, then read the delivery doc's last attempt for the verdict instead of tick counters.

verifiedOk: API key mint/hash/constant-time: sk_live_ + 32 base62 minted with crypto randomInt, sha-256 keyHash, timingSafeEqual verification (apikeys/core.ts:44-82); raw key returned exactly once at mint, only hash + 12-char prefix persisted (apikeys/store.ts:101-130, api-keys/actions.ts:80-92)

Guard chain on every public route: x-request-id echo, Bearer auth (constant-time + revocation + IP allowlist, null on any failure), per-key sliding-window rate limit with 429 + Retry-After + X-RateLimit headers, scope check, fire-and-forget usage $inc — in that order (apikeys/http.ts:75-121)

Usage sparkline is real: per-(key,minute) $inc upsert with 7-day TTL index (apikeys/store.ts:80-87,233-244), hourly 24h aggregation (store.ts:250-271), rendered in the api-keys detail drawer via keyUsageAction with an ownership check (api-keys/page.tsx:79-101,501; actions.ts:107-122)

All 6 public /api/v1/sms/* surfaces are genuine thin wrappers, no mocks: messages POST renders template/vars then sabsmsEngine.enqueueSend (messages/route.ts:108-194), messages GET + /:id are workspace-filtered Mongo reads with a narrow projection (no cost/provider internals — projection.ts:27-44), verify/send+check call engine otpSend/otpVerify (engine-client.ts:483-510) passing engine denial codes through (verify/engine-errors.ts), suppressions share the dashboard's hashPhone so both surfaces hit the same docs, analytics/summary reads sabsms_stats_daily rollups with range validation
